decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed because it exceeded the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material is already at its maximum effective conductivity, the next best lever is to increase the geometric cross-section of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat distribution. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing the local thermal resistance and lowering the peak temperature without violating material bounds.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Maintained feasibility regarding material property bounds
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the left/right domain boundaries; ensure it does not interfere with boundary condition application points if they are localized.
Geometric changes may require mesh adaptation in subsequent steps if element quality degrades near edges.
